comment.body:
and yepp, the same rule plays out here too: 
if the demand for a certain VIX bet is elevated, the implied vol of that bet will be bid , so SPX vomma will go up
if the demand decreases of that VIX bet, the implied vol of that bet will be offered, so SPX vomma will go down

comment.body: You have to also consider your account size. An /MES futures contract is about 50X leverage that of a single SPY share. Holding a /MES contract more than 23 hours would require about $1,600 in margin (unaccounted account balance). That would be the equivalent of holding about 2.66666666 SPY shares overnight. So for the same margin (cash) requirements, an /MES contract is about 18 times the leverage of SPY dollar for dollar. So if you had, say, $4,800 per contract of cash, then you'd cut down the leverage by 3X, and you'd have about 6X the leverage of SPY for that amount of money.
comment.raw_body: You have to also consider your account size. An /MES futures contract is about 50X leverage that of a single SPY share. comment.body: Now, as you mentioned, the nice thing about futures is no decay (but there can be if you are holding it for an extended period of time), and if you are in the US, futures are taxed at 60% long term capital gains and 40% short term. Options are 100% taxed as short term, so better for short term trades from a tax perpective. Now, if you had a large enough account balance to hold a longer term swing trade, there may be a chance for arbitrage (or decay) depending on where you believe the direction of the market is going and whether it is in contango or backwardation.
comment.raw_body: Now, as you mentioned, the nice thing about futures is no decay (but there can be if you are holding it for an extended period of time), and if you are in the US, futures are taxed at 60% long term capital gains and 40% short term. Options are 100% taxed as short term, so better for short term trades from a tax perpective. Now, if you had a large enough account balance to hold a longer term swing trade, there may be a chance for arbitrage (or decay) depending on where you believe the direction of the market is going and whether it is in contango or backwardation.

comment.body:
yes, it is like you are in a football cup and a bookmaker comes to you saying: "hey, wanna bet on the likelyhood that the likelyhood of the bet of XY on Les Bleus will go up?" 
would you bet on that? i guess you don't. It's sounds silly and fraudish.... but whenever somebody trades a VIX call, this is what he does. 
betting on something that is made up, that doesn't even exists.
But the wild thing is that across the forced hedging flows, this thing becomes self-fulfilling. this is why it is said that 'tail wagging the dog'.
think about that

comment.body:
and the key point is that derivatives generate tons of leverage.
somebody buys the stock. somebody bets on the stock price, somebody bet on the bet on the stock price, some body bets on the bet of the bet f the stock price... etc. 
huge leverage, money out of thin air. 
this is something you better understand!
